vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or entirely dormant/submerged. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are indistinguishable in the black pot. P3 (Pothos) is present but shows severe signs of decline.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot appears empty; soil surface shows no signs of active growth.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains only soil and debris; no identifiable mint or silver foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen shows a single, thin, chlorotic stem. The white rabbit anchor is present but the plant is failing to thrive.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Consistently dark and damp; potential for anaerobic conditions due to lack of transpiration.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of eggshell fragments used as a soil amendment/top dressing.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No moss or incidental growth detected; the environment appears sterile or suppressed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows a single green stem in the black pot (P3).
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-1: Gradual loss of turgor pressure in the P3 stem; progressive yellowing/browning.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Total collapse of the P3 stem; the plant is now essentially a necrotic remnant.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The lack of light (North window only) combined with the current soil moisture levels suggests root rot or total metabolic failure. The plants are not merely dormant; they are non-viable.
vision_report.anomalies: Eggshell fragments are the only non-plant intervention observed. The 'white rabbit' anchor is the only constant, highlighting the total loss of the surrounding biological subjects.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor desk biome. Over the 5-day observation period, the Pothos (P3) transitioned from a struggling, singular stem to a state of total collapse. The other registered specimens (P1, P2, P4) were never visually confirmed as active, suggesting they were either lost prior to the start of this sequence or are buried beneath the soil surface. The environment is currently devoid of healthy, photosynthesizing tissue.
